behavior_summary: Defines an NPC 'guardsman' with simple inspect behavior, proximity/area-entry handling, scheduled maintenance/respawn logic, and a multi-branch interaction (use/talk) tree influenced by world-state flags.
GUARD9::look produces a short descriptive reaction: a 'dead guardsman' line if the NPC is flagged dead, otherwise a 'guardsman' description.

U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea resets a dead guard (clears dead/combat and teleports them to a fixed location) or starts proximity reactions (spawns other behavior workers) when an actor enters the guard's proximity trigger.

U8P_GUARD9::schedule periodically spawns a maintenance worker; depending on that worker's result it can clear the dead flag and spawn a respawn routine, allowing automatic revival/reinitialization over time.

U8P_GUARD9::use implements the guarded conversation: it emits an initial bark, runs auxiliary helpers, computes a small counter from global frees (hydrosFree, pyrosFree) plus a hasHeart check, and then selects between an 'apocalypse/panic' shout, an undead-warning, or a full multi-choice dialog tree that sets/reads guard-specific flags (guard9Met, guard9Pissed, guard9Thanked).
notable_control_flow: GUARD9::look: conditional on Npc::isDead(self) -> choose one of two short bark strings -> suspend/return.

U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea: conditional on Npc::isDead(self) -> if dead: Npc::clrDead, Npc::clrInCombat, Npc::teleport to fixed coords; else: spawn proximity reaction methods (METHOD::091C and METHOD::15E7) and suspend.
U8P_GUARD9::schedule: spawn METHOD::143A and inspect the spawned result; if condition met then clear dead state and spawn METHOD::133F to reinitialize/respawn.

U8P_GUARD9::use: sequential actions (bark, spawn, helper calls) then compute a local counter from global flags; branch into three major outcomes (apocalypse panic, undead warning, or multi-choice conversation). The conversation branch builds a selection list, uses Item::ask, and updates globals (guard9Met, guard9Pissed, guard9Thanked) based on choices and random small-talk via urandom.
open_questions: Exact behavior and content of spawned METHOD::<id> routines (e.g., 091C, 15E7, 143A, 133F, 1090, 0BCF) — they appear to be helper behaviors (proximity reactions, respawn workers) but their full effects are not present here.
Precise meaning of hasHeart (used to decide undead vs non-undead branch) — naming suggests it marks 'has heart' vs 'undead', but the branch semantics are inferred.
Exact teleport coordinates and orientation used in enterFastArea are present in the binary but not human-friendly here; the gameplay intent (move to guard post) is inferred.
FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 semantics — appear to be utility routines invoked during use(), but their UI/side-effect specifics are unclear.
Whether guard9Thanked affects other systems beyond preventing repeated thank-you dialog (inferred to be a one-time flag).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD9::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short descriptive reaction when the player inspects the guard: say 'dead guardsman' if the NPC is flagged dead, otherwise say 'guardsman'.
entry_conditions: Event 0: the player (or actor) 'looks at' / inspects this NPC (pid passed in).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_GUARD9::look' / inspect event producing a short bark describing the guard or his corpse.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity/fast-area trigger: revive & teleport a dead guard or start proximity reaction behaviours for a live guard.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic maintenance/scheduler: spawn maintenance worker and possibly clear dead state and reinitialize guard behaviour (respawn).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::use
function_map.role: Handle direct player 'use' / talk: initial bark, auxiliary calls, evaluate world flags and local counter, then run either panic/undead lines or a multi-choice conversation that updates guard-specific globals.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to select the appropriate inspect/bark text.
Emit the inspect text ('dead guardsman' or 'guardsman') to the inspecting player (pid).
Decide whether to revive/reset the NPC (clear flags and teleport) or start proximity reactions.
Clear the NPC dead flag when reviving on area entry.
Clear combat state when reviving to stop combat behaviours.
Move the revived NPC to a fixed location/orientation (reset to guard post).
Start a proximity reaction behaviour for a nearby actor (inferred).
Start another proximity-related behaviour (inferred companion reaction).
Run a scheduled maintenance/worker to compute if the guard should be revived. Its return controls further steps.
Clear the dead flag if the scheduled worker indicates revival is needed.
Reinitialize or respawn behaviour after clearing dead state (inferred).
Emit the initial attention bark ('Guardsman!') and many subsequent dialog lines during conversation branches.
Start an auxiliary behaviour immediately after initial bark (function unknown; likely dialog/gesture helper).
Invoke a project-specific utility via spawn; likely a UI or auxiliary action used before branching (suspended on return).
Call a project-specific utility helper (not spawned).
Select randomized small-talk responses when guard9Pissed is false to vary dialog.
Present a multi-choice dialog list (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, Bye) and obtain player selection to navigate the conversation tree.
Spawn follow-up behaviour after major dialog branches (apocalypse/undead), inferred to animate or change AI state.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to a local counter in U8P_GUARD9::use that raises the likelihood of the 'apocalypse' panic branch; reflects whether a region/faction is 'freed'.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the same local counter used to choose the panic/apocalypse branch in use().

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Used in use() to pick an undead-related dialog branch vs a normal branch; inferred to indicate whether the player is living (hasHeart) or undead (no heart).

reads.name: guard9Met
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the player has previously met this guard; read to choose first-meeting vs follow-up dialog.

reads.name: guard9Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Controls curt vs normal dialog tone; read to pick dialog and written to set/reset the guard's annoyance state.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Influences answers to 'What is your duty?' and 'Where am I?' questions—represents broader world/NPC presence that changes text.

reads.name: guard9Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Read to avoid repeating a thank-you sequence; marks whether the guard already gave a one-time thanks (e.g., for ridding Mordea).

writes.name: guard9Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 during first successful 'use' interaction to mark that the guard has been met for future dialog branching.

writes.name: guard9Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set or cleared depending on the player's dialog choices or guard reactions; used to persist the guard's annoyed/calm state into future interactions.

writes.name: guard9Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the guard thanks the player for in-world actions; prevents repeating the same thank-you sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Defines an NPC 'guardsman' with simple inspect behavior, proximity/area-entry handling, scheduled maintenance/respawn logic, and a multi-branch interaction (use/talk) tree influenced by world-state flags.
